NotepadPlus.AutoComplete:
<Environment ignoreCase="yes" startFunc="(" stopFunc=")" paramSeparator="," terminal="" additionalWordChar="#<>%"/>
<Overload retVal="{Integer}" descr=" attr: Can be combined in any order and any case, e.g. 'raH'. a = FILE_ATTRIBUTE_ARCHIVE (32) h = FILE_ATTRIBUTE_HIDDEN (2) r = FILE_ATTRIBUTE_READONLY (1) s = FILE_ATTRIBUTE_SYSTEM (4)
mode: 0 = get 1 = set 2 = replace 3 = toggle 4 = remove
itemlist: List of items (full path) separated by or | to attrstamp. If empty then the currently selected list items are attrstamped.
returns: old attributes of the last processed item. The return value is made from summing up the attributes bit values. Example: RAH = 1 + 32 + 2 = 35.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" destination: The destination where the selected items should be backed up to. source: The item that will be copied or moved. Can be a list of items, separated by or |. Alternatively it can point to the source control (':tree', ':list', or ':catalog'), or to a text file containing a list of source items ('*filename'; see below).
If missing then the selected items of the focused control (List, Tree, Catalog) are used.">
<Param name="[source=]"/> <Param name="[target=]"/>
<Param name="[source=]"/> <Param name="[target=]"/>
<Param name="[source=]"/> <Param name="[target=]"/>
<Param name="[source=]"/> <Param name="[target=]"/>
<Param name="linebreaker=
"/>
<Param name="linebreaker=
"/>
<Param name="[term=]"/> <Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[term=]"/> <Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" file: The Catalog file to load. Can be relative to the folder (by default it is \Catalogs). Also portable paths ('?:\') are supported. If empty the default Catalog (\catalog.dat) is (re)loaded.
switches: Can be concatenated in any order. s = Save any changes in the current Catalog. p = Make [file] the permanent Catalog, i.e. load this Catalog on next app start. n = Create a new empty Catalog named [file]. i = Include (temporarily merge) an external catalog in the current one. e = Expand an included catalog. u = Unload an included catalog. missing = Defaults to 'sp'.
If i or u is present all other switches are ignored.">
<Param name="[location=]"/>
<Param name="[location=]"/>
<Param name="[soundfile=\Sounds (file|*1|*2|*EventLabel|'')]"/>
<Param name="[soundfile=\Sounds (file|*1|*2|*EventLabel|'')]"/>
<Param name="[soundfile=\Sounds (file|*1|*2|*EventLabel|'')]"/>
<Param name="[soundfile=\Sounds (file|*1|*2|*EventLabel|'')]"/>
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" file: File to save the column layout to, or to load it from. Either full path or resolved relative to \Columns\. If missing you are prompted by a standard Windows dialog. If no extension is passed the extension defaults to '.txt'.
mode: load = Load column layout from file. save = Save column layout to file. get = Just return the current column layout.
returns: the column layout loaded or saved.">
<Param name="items="/>
<Overload retVal="{Integer}" descr=" method: b = binary, bytes: strings are compared alphabetically. i = same as b, but case-insensitive n = numeric = strings are converted to numbers (fractions use dot) and then compared numerically. v = version = compares file versions of formats like #.## or #.##.#### or #.##.##.## d = date = compares dates, optionally including times; both string1 and string2. Default to the current date/time.
returns: -1 = string1 < string2 0 = string1 == string2 1 = string1 > string2
Valid date syntax depends on your locale. Fractions of a second (up to 7 decimal digits) are supported.">
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Overload retVal="{Integer}" descr=" ThumbsOverlaySpecs: These are various custom properties of the thumbnail overlay that's shown when Configuration | Preview | Thumbnails | Show dimensions of original is ticked. Syntax = TextColor (RRGGBB),BackColor (RRGGBB),Opaqueness (0-255),ThousandSeparator (0/1), WhichImageSize(0=original,1=embedded,2=both),FontSizePercentage (50-200) You can set certain values while leaving the others untouched.
ThumbsOverlayContent: Here you can define a custom content for the thumbnail overlay that's shown when Configuration | Preview | Thumbnails | Show dimensions of original is ticked. By default the dimensions of the original are shown, but you can show anything you like, and all XYplorer native variables are supported in your definition. Note that you can use for line breaks. You can specify a special section for video files. If the section marker |video:| is present in the definition, then everything after it is used for videos, everything before it is used for images.
returns: current value.">
<Param name="[LineBreaker=
]"/>
<Param name="[LineBreaker=
]"/>
<Param name="[LineBreaker=
]"/>
<Param name="[LineBreaker=
]"/>
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" Returns per Control:
Control Control Short Form List 1 L 1 ScrollX L 1 ScrollY L 1 List 2 L 2 ScrollX L 2 ScrollY L 2
Tree T ScrollX T ScrollY T
Catalog C ScrollY C Address Bar A Toolbar TB| Tab Bar 1 TAB 1| Tab Bar 2 TAB 2|
Breadcrumb 1 BC 1 Breadcrumb 2 BC 2 Status Bar SB Live Filter Box LFB Preview Pane PP
The first control has index 1. Separators count as buttons.">
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" Control Control Short Form List Active L List 1 L 1 ScrollX L 1 ScrollY L 1 List 2 L 2 ScrollX L 2 ScrollY L 2
Tree T ScrollX T ScrollY T
Catalog C ScrollY C Address Bar A Toolbar TB| Tab Bar 1 TAB 1| Tab Bar 2 TAB 2|
Breadcrumb 1 BC 1 Breadcrumb 2 BC 2 Status Bar SB Live Filter Box LFB Preview Pane PP
The first control has index 1. Separators count as buttons.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" ItemList: or | separated list of items (files or folders). Supports environment variables, XYplorer native variables, relative (to application path) and portable paths.
mode: cut = cut all files copy = copy all files empty = preserve mode for items that already in the clipboard.
append: 1 = Append itemlist to the items already in clipboard.
The command will only copy those items that do exist/are available in this moment. Other items are silently ignored. If none of the passed items are available you get a message. // No smartness whatsoever is applied when appending. So you can create crazy clipboard contents with items being present multiple times, containing each other, or from totally different locations.
You are in control.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" pattern: Name pattern. Can contain: * = file base ? = file extensio. Variables are allowed. empty = show prompt
TargetPath: Path to copy the items to. empty = use current list path. The path is silently auto-created if it does not exist.
ItemList: or | separated list of items (full path, or relative to current path) to copy.">
<Param name="[item=]"/> <Param name="[copy=-]"/>
<Param name="[item=]"/> <Param name="[copy=-]"/>
<Param name="[item=]"/> <Param name="[copy=-]"/>
<Param name="[item=]"/> <Param name="[copy=-]"/>
<Param name="[linebreaker='
']"/>
<Param name="[linebreaker='
']"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" destination: The destination where the selected items should be copied up to.
source: The item that will be copied or moved. Can be a list of items, separated by or |. Alternatively it can point to the source control (':tree', ':list', or ':catalog'), or to a text file containing a list of source items ('*filename'; see below). missing = selected items of the focused control (List, Tree, Catalog) are used.
rootpath: Common root for a rich operation (source paths are recreated in the target location relative to rootpath). Slashed or unslashed makes no difference.
flags: 0 = Default 1 = FilesOnly. For sources containing wildcards only files are copied. 2 = SkipPromptToCreate. Don't prompt, but create any non-existing destination folder without asking.
AutoRichOperation: This flag temporarily overwrites the global flag AutoRichFileOps. 0 = Ask 1 = Always 2 = Never">
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" If the script is called from outside a CTB you have to state a button index, else you will get an error message. The new icon set via scripting is treated just like an icon set via UI; so it is as well remembered across sessions. You can even set icons of buttons that are not shown in the current toolbar.
icon: Whatever can be entered into the Icon field in a CTB definition, e.g.: - Path to an icon resource (ICO, ICL, EXE, or DLL file). Full path or relative to . - Toolbar icon key of XYplorer's internal toolbar icons; e.g. ':home'. empty = returns the current icon.">
<Param name="[date=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr="Begins saving debug logs to a logfile in . {Undocumented}">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" recycle: 1 = use Recycle Bin 0 = no Recycle Bin
confirm: 1 = confirm prompt 0 = no confirm prompt empty = user setting in configuration
ItemList: or | separated list of items (full path) to delete. :tree = delete current tree selection :list = delete current list selection(s) empty = current Tree or List selections are deleted, depending on the focus.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" url: Any URL (http://, https://, or ftp://)
TargetFile: Full path/file of desired target; UNC works as well Defaults: file = \TargetFile path = \sourceurlfilename relative = \TargetFile no file = 'Download[current date].htm' empty = \SourceUrlFileName
options: o = On collision: overwrite without asking. i = On collision: auto-increment targetfile. b = Check byte count after download. On no-match the download fails. s = Silent: No status bar progress and success messages. empty = On collision: ask before overwriting.">
<Param name="[linebreaker=
]"/>
<Param name="[linebreaker=
]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[file1=]"/> <Param name="[file2=]"/>
<Param name="[file1=]"/> <Param name="[file2=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" AND: Boolean AND is supported. The operator can be AND or & (both surrounded by spaces; AND is not case-sensitive): cat AND dog AND pig = all of these animals must be in the name cat & dog & pig = all of these animals must be in the name water and ageM: w = 'water' in the name, modified this week
size: < 1KB AND *.txt; size: < 100 AND *.gif = TXT < 1 KB, or GIF < 100 bytes 'Image Files 2013' {:Image} and 2013 = image files with '2013' in the name">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" Captions: You can also define a caption for each Visual Filter. Simply prefix a quoted string followed by at least one space to the filter: 'Images' *.gif | *.png | *.jpg 'Items ending with g' >g$
The tab captions will show only the (unquoted) caption of a Visual Filter with caption. The caption of a Visual Filter can have a short and a long version. The short and the long version are separated by | '30 Mins|Modified In The Last 30 Mins' ageM: <= 30 n '3 Hours|Modified In The Last 3 Hours' ageM: <= 3 h 'Today|Modified Today' ageM: d 'Week|Modified This Week' ageM: w 'Year|Modified This Year' ageM: y">
<Param name="[SepReturn=]"/>
<Param name="[SepReturn=]"/>
<Param name="[SepReturn=]"/>
<Param name="[SepReturn=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/>
<Param name="[folder=]"/> <Param name="[template='||' (|||)]"/>
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" folder: Folder to investigate.
template: Format of the returned string. Each <..> replaced with: = folder count. = file count. = byte count (formatted, rounded up). = byte count (raw, exact).
recursive: 0 = non-recursive 1 = recursive, i.e. including subfolders
returns: The string with info formatted according to the specified template.
Can take time with huge folders. The process can be stopped by ESC."> <Param name="[folder=]"/> <Param name="[template='||' (|||)]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" For-loops are internally converted to While loops:
$i = 1; for ($i = 1; $i <= 3; $i++) { while ($i <= 3) { echo $i; -> echo $i; $i++; } }
The values '' and '0' evaluate to 0 (false). All other values evaluates to 1 (true). Parens. ( ) braces { } and semicolon ; are mandatory. Spaces and new lines are optional:
for(..){statement;} for (..) { statement; } are the same.
The following is wrong: for .. {..} for (..) statement;">
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" Date expressions should be formatted either in the current locale, or in the universal ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD hh:nn:ss).
Examples: text format(1, '000'); //001 text format(1, '@@@'); // 1 (leading spaces) text format(1234.56, '#,0.00'); //1,234.56 text format(, 'yyyy'); //2010 text format(, 'dd-mmm-yy'); //20-Sep-10 text format(, 'dddd'); //Monday (in your locale)">
<Overload retVal="{String}" descr=" create a number of empty TXT files in the current folder $numfiles = input('Number of files to create', , '10'); $i = 1; while ($i <= $numfiles) { new('file' . format($i, '000') . '.txt'); $i++; }">
<Param name="[date=]"/>
<Overload retVal="{Date}" descr=" Returns a date/time expression in a specific format, optionally shifted by a specific interval.
date: Date expression to be formatted (should be formatted either in the current locale, or in the universal ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD hh:nn:ss).
format: date format: yyyymmdd_hhnnss, dddd ... Supports the named format ISOWeek See also
ShiftUnit: Unit to shift date by y = years m = months w = weeks d = days h = hours n = minutes s = seconds Legal results: between 01.01.0100 and 31.12.9999
ShiftNum: number of units to shift date by; negative to go back in time; must be an integer value.
returns: String with formatted date."> <Param name="[date=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" Start path in the first pane will be set to the .
Fresh mode sets the Application Data Path to subfolder 'Fresh' of the normal Application Data Path. This way it's impossible that any of your everyday settings are inadvertently overwritten by the fresh instance.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" You can pass an optional format template in the 3rd argument after the property selector. The * is a place holder for the return value (if missing the value is simply appended to the template). Must be wrapped in single or double quotes if a space is included:
Bytes: 1,436,919 1,436,919 Bytes Duration [01:00:52] Duration: 01:00:52">
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[tagslist=]"/>
<Param name="[tagslist=]"/>
<Param name="[tagslist=]"/>
<Param name="[tagslist=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" file: Full path to the file / relative to the directory / filename without extension. Extension defaults to 'xys' (if not specified). Quotes are optional but might look better.
Evaluated unconditionally when a script resource is loaded and before anything else is done. You cannot use any script variables for file. Only XYplorer variables and environment variables are supported. The include statement must be on its own line. Other statements can be on the same line even after semicolon ;
Include statements can be nested (included files can themselves include other files). The maximum nesting level for Include statements is 100 (one hundred). You will get an error if you go beyond. Beware of recursion: A file must not include itself or any file by which it has been included, else you will reach the maximum nesting level within the next millisecond...
Include statements can be indented. Included file will inherit the indent of the include statement.">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" file: Full path to the file / relative to the directory / filename without extension. Extension defaults to 'xys' (if not specified). Quotes are optional but might look better.
Evaluated unconditionally when a script resource is loaded and before anything else is done. You cannot use any script variables for file. Only XYplorer variables and environment variables are supported. The include statement must be on its own line. Other statements can be on the same line even after semicolon ;
Trying to execute 'Include_Once' again an already included file will simply ignore the statement and continue without eny errors.
Include statements can be nested (included files can themselves include other files). The maximum nesting level for Include statements is 100 (one hundred). You will get an error if you go beyond. Beware of recursion: A file must not include itself or any file by which it has been included, else you will reach the maximum nesting level within the next millisecond...
Include statements can be indented. Included file will inherit the indent of the include statement.">
<Param name="[path=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr="Create logs of certain app events to a file in , or disable logging. {Undocumented}">
<Overload retVal="" descr=" pattern: Name pattern. Can contain: * = file base ? = file extensio. Variables are allowed. empty = show prompt
targetpath: Path to copy the items to. empty = use current list path. The path is silently auto-created if it does not exist.
itemlist: or | separated list of items (full path, or relative to current path) to copy.">
<Overload retVal="" descr="location: The destination where the selected items should be moved/copied/backed up to. source: The item that will be copied or moved. Can be a list of items, separated by or |. Alternatively it can point to the source control (':tree', ':list', or ':catalog'), or to a text file containing a list of source items ('*filename'; see below).
If missing then the selected items of the focused control (List, Tree, Catalog) are used.
rootpath: Common root for a rich operation (source paths are recreated in the target location relative to rootpath). Slashed or unslashed makes no difference.
flags: 0 = Default 1 = FilesOnly. For sources containing wildcards only files are copied. 2 = SkipPromptToCreate. Don't prompt, but create any non-existing destination folder without asking.
autorichop: 0 = Ask 1 = Always 2 = Never This flag temporarily overwrites the global flag AutoRichFileOps.">
<Param name="[linebreaker='
']"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[item=]"/>
<Overload retVal="" descr=" item: File or folder to pop the shell context menu for. Can also be a list of items separated by or | (the separators must not be mixed!) The items can be located in different places (Win7+).
bitness {Obsolete}: Bitness of the shown menu. XY v28.00+: only the 64-bit menu is shown.
flags: 1 = hide shell extensions (see Remarks).
The menu is displayed at the mouse cursor. Hide shell extensions: The effect is identical to ticking 'Configuration | General | Menus, Mouse, Usability | Context Menus | Hide shell extensions from shell context menu'."> <Param name="[item=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[itemlist=]"/>
<Param name="[sourceitem=]"/>
<Param name="[base=]"/>
<Param name="[file=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[separator=]"/>
<Param name="[linebreaker='
']"/>
